title: The Strategy of Fabianism
author: From: Quintus Fabius Maximus (History)
content: Fabius defeated Hannibal not by fighting him, but by exhausting him through patience and tactical attrition. Time can be a weapon if you have the discipline to wait.
action: Outlast a difficult problem through consistent attrition.

title: The Principle of Negative Entropy
author: From: Erwin Schrödinger
content: Living things maintain their structure by constantly drawing energy from the environment. Your project will decay unless you actively feed it new ideas and maintenance.
action: Inject new information to disrupt stagnation.

title: On the Sovereignty of the Individual
author: From: John Stuart Mill
content: The only purpose for which power can be rightfully exercised over any member of a civilized community, against his will, is to prevent harm to others.
action: Shift influence tactics from power to persuasion.

title: The OODA Loop: Orientation
author: From: John Boyd
content: Orientation is the process of filtering incoming information through your cultural, genetic, and experience-based lenses. If your orientation is wrong, your decisions will be wrong.
action: Adjust mental filters to correct recurring biases.

title: The Transcontinental Railroad
author: From: American History
content: The project was a failure of management but a triumph of persistence. It redefined what the 'frontier' meant by physically bridging the gap.
action: Build a bridge toward your current project frontier.
